Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Rolin Smth S Further Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two, Statement Of Disputed Responses, And Request For Sanctions, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion, Declaration Of Ryan S. Cowan, [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Dec-09-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Ross McKissick, Esq. (415.495.0440; ross@harrispersonalinjury.com) of Harris Personal Injury Lawyers, Inc. for Plaintiff.
Ryan S. Cowan, Esq. (415.403.4449; rcowan@eghblaw.com) of Edlin Gallagher Huie + Blum for Defendant/Moving Party.
Caitlin Mitchell, Esq. (707.559.6701; cmitchell@wshblaw.com) of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP for Defendants, Uber Technologies, Inc., et al. (observing only).

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARIO SILVA GAVAO's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Rolin Smith's Further Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two, Statement Of Disputed Responses, And Request For Sanctions:

The motion is granted as follows: plaintiff's responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 are not full and complete, as required. Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 2030.360(d). General reference to other documents for responsive information is insufficient. See Deyo v. Killbourne, 84 Cal.App.3d 771 (1978). Plaintiff must instead specify the documents in in sufficient detail to ascertain the answer. Code of Civ. Proc. Sec. 2030.230. Plaintiff shall serve further code-compliant responses to said Form Interrogatory. Plaintiff's counsel Ross McKissick and Plaintiff Rolin Smith are jointly ordered to pay sanctions of $1,000 to Defendant Mario Siva Gavao. Further responses and sanction are due within 20 days of the date of order.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MINI MINUTES:

Ross McKissick, Esq. (415.495.0440; ross@harrispersonalinjury.com) of Harris Personal Injury Lawyers, Inc. for Plaintiff.
Ryan S. Cowan, Esq. (415.403.4449; rcowan@eghblaw.com) of Edlin Gallagher Huie + Blum for Defendant/Moving Party.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARIO SILVA GAVAO's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Rolin Smith's Further Verified Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, To Produce Documents And A Privilege Log, Request For Monetary Sanctions:

The motion is granted as follows: Plaintiff's objections to requests for production Nos. 1 through 5 are overruled as unsupported boilerplate except as to privilege and privacy. As to information claimed to be privileged, Plaintiff shall serve a code-compliant privilege log. The requests for production are relevant to the subject matter and reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence such that any privacy interests therein are outweighed by Defendant's interest in full disclosure of relevant information. Plaintiff's purported reservations of a right to amend is inconsistent with the Discovery Act and are therefore stricken. Plaintiff's responses are not full and complete as required. Plaintiff shall serve further code-compliant responses to said requests for production. Plaintiff's counsel Ross McKissick and Plaintiff Rolin Smith are jointly ordered to pay sanctions of $1,000 to Defendant Mario Siva Gavao. Privilege log, further responses and sanctions are due within 20 days of the date of order.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARIO SILVA GAVAO's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Rolin Smith's Further Verified Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Sanctions:

The motion is granted as follows: Plaintiff's objections to special interrogatories nos. 18, 26, 27, 67, and 68 are overruled as unsupported boilerplate except as to privilege and privacy. As to information claimed to be privileged, Plaintiff shall serve a code-compliant privilege log. The requests for production are relevant to the subject matter and reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence such that any privacy interests therein are outweighed by Defendant's interest in full disclosure of relevant information. Plaintiff's purported reservations of a right to amend is inconsistent with the Discovery Act and are therefore stricken. Plaintiff's responses fail to disclose whether he has made a good faith effort to ascertain the information sought from sources reasonably within his control and are therefore full and complete as required. Plaintiff shall serve further code-compliant responses to said special interrogatories. Plaintiff's counsel Ross McKissick and Plaintiff Rolin Smith are jointly ordered to pay sanctions of $1,000 to Defendant Mario Siva Gavao. Privilege log, further responses and sanctions are due within 20 days of the date of order.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to jameshfleming@gmail.com.
